calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: Q-017 control-axis minimal subset: FULL_AXES vs COLLAPSED_SCALAR vs MINIMAL_SUBSET (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0108 (EVB-0081): Q-017 asks what is the minimal orthogonal control-axis subset that preserves observed regime separations. Three conditions: FULL_AXES (3 axes: z_tonic=EMA(harm,tau=50), z_phasic=|harm-z_tonic|, z_affect=harm-benefit), COLLAPSED_SCALAR (1 axis: raw harm_signal -- null hypothesis that decomposition is unnecessary), MINIMAL_SUBSET (2 axes: z_tonic+z_phasic, no affective axis -- tests whether benefit channel is dispensable). Policy: Linear(z_world+z_self+control_dim, 64)+ReLU+Linear(64, action_dim)+entropy bonus. Harm evaluator: Linear(z_world+z_self, 1). Regimes inferred per WINDOW_SIZE=20 steps: AVOIDANCE (mean_harm>0.03), EXPLOITATION (resource_count>0, harm<=0.03), EXPLORATION (harm<=0.03, no resources). Regime separation = |harm_avoidance - harm_exploration| + |ent_exploitation - ent_exploration|. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 FULL_AXES regime_sep > COLLAPSED + 0.02 both seeds; C2 FULL_AXES regime_sep > 0.01 both seeds; C3 >=5 windows per regime per condition per seed; C4 all three axis variances > 1e-5 both seeds; C5 seed-consistent direction for FULL vs MINIMAL gap. PASS: all three axes load-bearing. PARTIAL_TWO_SUFFICIENT: FULL~MINIMAL (|gap|<=0.02), affective axis dispensable. PARTIAL_COLLAPSE_ADEQUATE: raw harm sufficient at V3 scale. FAIL: insufficient regime events. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=3 hazard_harm=0.1 env_drift_interval=10 env_drift_prob=0.3. 400 total episodes, 100 measurement, 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (~264 min estimate).

items.title: Q-001 entity-binding pair: ENTITY_BINDING_ON vs ENTITY_ABLATED (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0086/EVB-0070: Q-001 entity emergence and binding. Tests whether z_self attentional gating (self_maintenance_weight=0.1) is the operative mechanism for entity persistence in z_world. ENTITY_BINDING_ON: z_self active, self_maintenance_weight=0.1. ENTITY_ABLATED: z_self zeroed after each sense(), no maintenance gradient. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 BINDING entity_event_response>=0.05, C2 ABLATED entity_event_response<=0.15, C3 per-seed event_response gap>=0.02, C4 BINDING persistence>=0.70, C5 per-seed persistence gap>=0.05. Entity events detected via obs_world delta>0.02 on env drift steps. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 drift_prob=0.3. 2 seeds [42,123]. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed. Low priority (priority=4) per Q-001 designation.

items.title: MECH-128/Q-002 E1 goal-conditioning pair: GOAL_CONDITIONED vs GOAL_ABLATED (seeds [42,123], world_dim=32+16)
items.note: EXP-0088 (covering MECH-128 and Q-002): Validates that E1 LSTM goal-conditioning (MECH-128) shapes E1 predictive distribution. GOAL_CONDITIONED: config.e1.goal_dim=world_dim (goal_input_proj active, z_goal passed to E1). GOAL_ABLATED: config.e1.goal_dim=0 (goal_input_proj disabled, z_goal withheld). Key metric: interaction term = (neutral_err_ablated - neutral_err_cond) - (goal_err_ablated - goal_err_cond). Positive interaction = goal context selectively reduces goal-relevant prediction error (MECH-128). Secondary Q-002 probe: world_dim=32 vs world_dim=16 for R-field resolution guidance. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 interaction_32>=0.005 (both seeds), C2 err_goal_cond < err_goal_abla (both seeds, dim=32), C3 neutral_diff_32 < 0.03 (specificity, both seeds), C4 err_all_32 <= err_all_16*1.10 (Q-002 resolution), C5 interaction_16>=0.003 (replication). CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources. 2 seeds [42,123]. 400 warmup + 120 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed. 4 conditions total (COND/ABLA x dim=32/16).

items.title: Q-004 tau_R calibration pair: FAST_TAU vs SLOW_TAU vs DEFAULT_TAU (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0092 / EVB-0073: Q-004 tau_R calibration discriminative triple. Tests whether tau_R (residue field effective integration timescale) should be calibrated to E2 forward-model horizon (~30 steps, FAST_TAU: accum_rate=0.5, bw=0.5) or E1 LSTM integration horizon (~200 steps, SLOW_TAU: accum_rate=0.02, bw=2.0), with DEFAULT_TAU (accum_rate=0.1, bw=1.0) as baseline. Key metric: mean_harm over 100 eval episodes x 200 steps. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 FAST_TAU mean_harm < SLOW_TAU * 0.90 (both seeds), C2 SLOW_TAU mean_harm < FAST_TAU * 0.90 (both seeds), C3 DEFAULT_TAU ranks between FAST and SLOW (monotone ordering), C4 FAST_TAU residue_contrast > 0.05 (RBF field builds usable signal), C5 seed consistency. PASS (C1+C4+C5) => calibrate to E2; PASS (C2+C4+C5) => calibrate to E1; PARTIAL (C4 only) => tau_R robust in range; FAIL (C4 fails) => accumulation pathological. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources, hazard_harm=0.05. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps x 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells.

items.title: Q-003 R-field dimensionality pair: SCALAR vs VECTOR (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0090 (EVB-0072): Q-003 asks whether R(x,t) should be scalar or vector. SCALAR condition uses standard ResidueField.evaluate() (1-D output). VECTOR condition uses VectorResidueWrapper.evaluate_vector() producing 3 dims: harm_intensity (standard RBF), harm_recency (EMA-decayed weights), benefit_proximity (ARC-030 benefit terrain). E3 receives vector fused into z_world via Linear(world_dim+3, world_dim) projection trained jointly. Key discriminator: harm_ratio = mean_harm_vector / mean_harm_scalar. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 harm_ratio<0.92 (vector reduces harm >=8%, both seeds), C2 dim_var>0.01 dims 0+1 (non-degenerate vector, both seeds), C3 benefit_dim2_mean>0.01 (benefit dim non-zero, both seeds), C4 harm_ratio<1.10 (no regression, both seeds), C5 C1 seed-consistent. PASS (C1+C2+C3): vector preferred. FAIL (C4): scalar preferred. FAIL (C2): inconclusive/collapsed. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources, drift_interval=10. 2 seeds [42,123]. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed.

items.title: Q-005 sleep annealing of R(x,t): NO_ANNEAL vs ANNEAL vs RESET (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0094 (EVB-0074): Q-005 asks whether offline integration can be modeled as annealing or resetting R(x,t). Three conditions: NO_ANNEAL (residue persists, current default), ANNEAL (RBF weights *= 0.3 after each waking phase, synaptic homeostasis analog), RESET (RBF weights zeroed, tabula rasa). Protocol: 3 waking phases x 100 episodes x 200 steps separated by 2 sleep phases. Primary metric: mean_harm_post_sleep (mean over waking phases 2+3 -- after first sleep). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 ANNEAL harm < NO_ANNEAL * 0.92 (both seeds), C2 RESET harm < NO_ANNEAL * 1.15 (no catastrophic regression, both seeds), C3 ANNEAL residue_contrast > 0.03 (field retains structure after anneal, both seeds), C4 NO_ANNEAL residue_contrast > 0.02 (persistent field builds spatial contrast, both seeds), C5 seed-consistent direction. PASS (C1+C3+C5): ANNEAL beneficial; offline partial R(x,t) decay is load-bearing. PARTIAL (NO_ANNEAL wins): persistent residue preferred; no offline op needed. FAIL (C3/C4 fails): residue field does not build/retain usable structure. 2 seeds x 3 conditions = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 2 hazards 3 resources hazard_harm=0.05.

items.title: Q-006 ethics developmental vs additive: SEQUENTIAL vs LATE_ADD vs FULL_ABLATION (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0096 (EVB-0075): Q-006 asks whether ethics is developmental or additive. Three conditions: SEQUENTIAL (harm supervision from episode 1, developmental), LATE_ADD (harm supervision from episode 75/150, additive with equal total budget), FULL_ABLATION (no harm supervision, floor). Primary metric: mean_harm in final 30 episodes. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 SEQUENTIAL harm < LATE_ADD * 0.90 (>=10% advantage, both seeds), C2 both ethics conditions beat FULL_ABLATION harm by >=5% (both seeds), C3 SEQUENTIAL residue_contrast > 0.03 in final eval window (both seeds), C4 LATE_ADD residue_contrast > 0.02 (both seeds), C5 C1 direction seed-consistent. PASS (C1+C2+C3+C5): ethics is developmental -- early integration better. PARTIAL_ADDITIVE_ADEQUATE (not C1, C2+C4+C5): additive ethics adequate. PARTIAL_TIMING_MATTERS (C1+C2a+not C2b): sequential wins but late addition insufficient. FAIL (not C3 or not C2a): implementation problem. 2 seeds x 3 conditions = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 2 hazards 3 resources hazard_harm=0.05 150 episodes x 200 steps each.

items.title: Q-012 control constraints necessary: REE_FULL vs PREDICTIVE_ONLY vs NO_LEARNING (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0098 (EVB-0076): Q-012 asks whether latent predictive world models can stay agentically stable without explicit REE-like control constraints. Three conditions: REE_FULL (E1+E3 harm eval+residue -- full REE pipeline), PREDICTIVE_ONLY (E1 only -- JEPA-style, no harm supervision), NO_LEARNING (random policy -- floor). Primary metric: mean_harm in final 30 episodes. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 REE_FULL harm < PREDICTIVE_ONLY * 0.85 (>=15% advantage, both seeds), C2 REE_FULL harm < NO_LEARNING * 0.80 (>=20% above floor, both seeds), C3 PREDICTIVE_ONLY does NOT consistently beat floor by >10% (control constraints necessary), C4 REE_FULL harm_variance not worse than PREDICTIVE_ONLY * 1.10, C5 seed-consistent. PASS (C1+C2+C3+C5): control constraints necessary. PARTIAL_PRED_ADEQUATE (not C1, C2+C5): predictive model adequate; JEPA argument has traction. PARTIAL_BOTH_FAIL (not C2): underpowered or env harm insufficient. FAIL: REE_FULL not beating random floor; implementation problem. 2 seeds x 3 conditions. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 3 hazards 2 resources hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=10 env_drift_prob=0.3. 150 episodes x 200 steps each.

items.title: Q-013 uncertainty calibration: DETERMINISTIC_DISPERSION vs STOCHASTIC_HEAD (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0100 (EVB-0077): Q-013 asks whether deterministic JEPA plus derived dispersion can match explicit stochastic uncertainty heads for REE precision routing. Two conditions: DETERMINISTIC_DISPERSION (plain linear encoder + running z_world std over last 10 steps as uncertainty signal, no KL), STOCHASTIC_HEAD (mean+log_var encoder, reparametrized z_world, sigma=exp(0.5*log_var), KL_WEIGHT=0.001). Both trained with E1 world prediction loss only (no E3/harm supervision -- uncertainty must emerge from world-model training alone). Calibration metric: AUC of uncertainty signal predicting harm in next 5 steps. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 STOCHASTIC_HEAD calibration_auc >= 0.58 both seeds; C2 DETERMINISTIC_DISPERSION calibration_auc >= 0.58 both seeds; C3 STOCHASTIC gap >= 0.05 over DETERMINISTIC both seeds; C4 n_harm_events >= 30 per condition per seed (data quality); C5 seed-consistent direction. PASS (C1+C3+C4+C5): stochastic heads superior; hybridize recommendation supported. PARTIAL_DET_ADEQUATE (C2+C4+C5+not C3): deterministic dispersion adequate at this scale. PARTIAL_BOTH_POOR (not C1, not C2): neither calibrated; inconclusive. FAIL: insufficient harm events or degenerate AUC. 2 seeds x 2 conditions = 4 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 4 hazards 0 resources hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.3. 200 warmup + 50 eval episodes x 200 steps each.

items.title: Q-014 JEPA invariance blind spot: JEPA_INVARIANT vs HARM_SENSITIVE (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0102 (EVB-0078): Q-014 asks whether JEPA invariances hide ethically relevant distinctions in REE attribution contexts. Two conditions: JEPA_INVARIANT (stop-gradient target encoder EMA tau=0.99, predictor-only gradient flow, VICReg collapse prevention, no event supervision), HARM_SENSITIVE (event cross-entropy auxiliary loss + world prediction loss, full encoder gradient). Key metric: attribution_auc = AUC of post-hoc linear probe decoding agent-caused vs env-caused harm from z_world. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 HARM_SENSITIVE attribution_auc >= 0.65 both seeds; C2 JEPA_INVARIANT attribution_auc >= 0.65 both seeds; C3 HARM_SENSITIVE exceeds JEPA_INVARIANT by >= 0.08 both seeds (blind-spot confirmed); C4 n_agent_harm >= 20 and n_env_harm >= 20 per condition per seed (data quality); C5 seed-consistent direction. PASS (C1+C3+C4+C5): JEPA invariance suppresses attribution signal; hybridize recommendation supported. PARTIAL_BOTH_HIGH (C2+C4+C5+not C3): JEPA preserves attribution at this scale; blind-spot not confirmed. PARTIAL_BOTH_LOW (not C1, not C2): neither encoder achieves attribution calibration; inconclusive. FAIL: insufficient harm events or implementation problem. 2 seeds x 2 conditions = 4 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 5 hazards 0 resources hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=3 env_drift_prob=0.4. 300 train + 100 probe episodes x 200 steps each. Mac-only (192 min estimate; ~5x slower on Daniel-PC).

items.title: Q-015 commit boundary minimal contract: WITH_BOUNDARY vs BLENDED vs MINIMAL_BOUNDARY (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0104 (EVB-0079): Q-015 asks what the smallest commit-boundary token is that still supports reliable multi-timescale attribution. Three conditions: WITH_BOUNDARY (binary gate, episode phase split at step 50%, pre-commit=world_pred_loss only, post-commit=harm_eval_loss only), BLENDED (no gate, alpha=0.5 fixed mixing of both losses at every step), MINIMAL_BOUNDARY (soft learned gate g=sigmoid(MLP(z_world,z_self)), loss=g*harm_eval+(1-g)*world_pred, no external phase info). Key metrics: last_q_harm (mean harm last 100 episodes), pre_post_corr (|Pearson r| between pre-commit world_pred_error and post-commit harm_eval_error), gate_entropy (MINIMAL_BOUNDARY only, near-0 = near-binary convergence). 7 pre-registered criteria: C1 WITH_BOUNDARY last_q_harm < BLENDED - 0.05 both seeds (MECH-061 replication at V3); C2 WITH_BOUNDARY pre_post_corr < 0.30 both seeds (signal separation achieved); C3 BLENDED pre_post_corr > 0.25 both seeds (confirms separation is a boundary effect); C4 MINIMAL_BOUNDARY last_q_harm < BLENDED - 0.05 both seeds; C5 |MINIMAL_BOUNDARY - WITH_BOUNDARY| last_q_harm < 0.05 both seeds (equivalent performance); C6 MINIMAL_BOUNDARY pre_post_corr < 0.30 both seeds; C7 n_harm_events >= 30 per condition per seed (data quality). PASS (all criteria): minimal soft gate is sufficient, binary boundary not required. PARTIAL_FULL_NEEDED (C1+C2+C3, not C4/C5/C6): strict binary boundary required at V3 scale. PARTIAL_V3_FAIL (not C1): MECH-061 does not replicate at V3 scale. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.3. 400 total episodes, 100 measurement episodes (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (264 min estimate; ~5x slower on Daniel-PC).

items.title: Q-016 tri-loop arbitration policy: PRIORITY_HARM vs WEIGHTED_SUM vs WINNER_TAKE_ALL vs BLENDED (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0106 (EVB-0080): Q-016 asks what arbitration policy best resolves tri-loop gate conflicts without coupling collapse. Four conditions: PRIORITY_HARM (motivational gate overrides at conflict steps; mean of all three otherwise), WEIGHTED_SUM (fixed linear blend: 0.2*motor + 0.3*cog + 0.5*mot), WINNER_TAKE_ALL (argmax gate wins, others contribute 0), BLENDED baseline (no gating, 0.5*harm + 0.5*world_pred loss). Three loop gates trained jointly: motor (body-state input), cognitive (world-state input), motivational (joint input). Conflict = |g_motor - g_cog| > 0.15. Key metrics: harm_rate (mean harm in last 125 episodes), inter_loop_spread (mean pairwise absolute difference between gate values at conflict steps -- higher = more loop independence), commit_gate_variance (gate informativeness), n_conflict_steps (data quality gate). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 best-policy harm_rate < BLENDED - 0.03 both seeds; C2 best-policy inter_loop_spread > 0.05 both seeds (no coupling collapse); C3 WTA inter_loop_spread > WEIGHTED_SUM both seeds (directional: hard inhibition preserves loop independence better than blending); C4 n_conflict_steps >= 50 per gated condition per seed; C5 best-policy commit_gate_variance > 0.01 both seeds (gate informative). PASS: winning policy identified. PARTIAL_NO_WINNER: no policy achieves both harm avoidance and loop independence. PARTIAL_COLLAPSE_ONLY: all policies collapse. FAIL: insufficient conflict events. 4 conditions x 2 seeds = 8 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=5 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.1 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.4. 500 total episodes, 125 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (440 min estimate).

items.title: Q-018 RC-conflict threshold calibration: LOW_THRESH vs HIGH_THRESH vs ADAPTIVE_THRESH (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0110 (EVB-0082): Q-018 asks what RC-conflict threshold calibration blocks authority spoofing without chronic over-suppression. Three conditions: LOW_THRESH (rc=0.05, fixed), HIGH_THRESH (rc=0.40, fixed), ADAPTIVE_THRESH (EMA+2*sigma). Authority spoofing injected on 10% of measurement steps (SPOOF_MAGNITUDE=3.0). Key metrics: discrimination_gap (suppression_rate_spoofing - suppression_rate_clean), clean_suppression_rate, harm_rate. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 ADAPTIVE disc_gap>=0.10 both seeds; C2 ADAPTIVE clean_rate < LOW clean_rate - 0.05 both seeds; C3 ADAPTIVE harm_rate <= HIGH harm_rate + 0.02 both seeds; C4 n_spoofing >= 30 per condition per seed; C5 LOW_THRESH disc_gap > 0 (sanity). PASS: EMA+sigma adaptive calibration blocks spoofing and avoids over-suppression; Q-018 partially resolved. PARTIAL_ADAPTIVE_DISC: discrimination good but over-suppression persists. PARTIAL_TRADEOFF: fundamental tension. FAIL: mechanism broken. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.08 env_drift_interval=8 env_drift_prob=0.3. 400 total episodes, 100 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (264 min estimate).

items.title: Q-020 ARC-007 valence constraint: VALUE_FLAT vs VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE vs VALUE_LADEN (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0112 (EVB-0083): Q-020 asks whether ARC-007's no-value-computation constraint survives MECH-073 (valence intrinsic to hippocampal map). Three conditions: VALUE_FLAT (residue-field shaping of candidate proposals, no direct valence head; ARC-007 compliant), VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE (ablation: no residue, no direct valence -- identifies whether residue is the mechanism), VALUE_LADEN (learned ValenceHead scores candidates during proposal generation before E3 sees them; MECH-073 analog). Key metrics: harm_rate (primary), n_harm_events (data quality), proposal_harm_score_mean (valence head activity). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 |VALUE_LADEN harm_rate - VALUE_FLAT harm_rate| <= 0.05 (parity, both seeds); C2 VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE harm_rate >= VALUE_FLAT harm_rate + 0.04 (residue benefit confirmed, both seeds); C3 VALUE_LADEN harm_rate <= 0.40 (not at floor); C4 n_harm_events >= 20 per condition per seed; C5 VALUE_LADEN proposal_harm_score_mean > 0.01 (valence head active). PASS (C1+C2+C3+C4+C5): ARC-007 constraint survives -- value-flat + residue achieves parity with value-laden; residue field is the operative mechanism. PARTIAL_LADEN_ADVANTAGE: VALUE_LADEN outperforms VALUE_FLAT beyond parity margin; direct valence adds value; ARC-007 may need revision. PARTIAL_NO_RESIDUE_EFFECT: residue ablation does not worsen VALUE_FLAT; confound at this scale. FAIL: insufficient harm events or valence head inactive. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=5 num_resources=0 hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.35. 500 total episodes, 125 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (330 min estimate).
